items.title: Remove unused SM2 algorithm and related configuration
items.body:
## Background

The java-tron codebase contains a complete SM2 implementation in the `crypto` module. The `SignUtils.java` provides a configuration flag `isECKeyCryptoEngine` to switch between ECKey (SECP256K1) and SM2. Additionally, SM3 hash implementation exists alongside SM2.

The `crypto.engine` configuration option allows switching between cryptographic engines, but in practice only ECKey is ever used in production.

## Problem Statement

1. **SM2/SM3 is never used in production**: All production deployments use `ECKey (SECP256K1)`. The `isECKeyCryptoEngine` flag is always `true`.

2. **Dead code maintenance burden**: Maintaining ~1,100+ lines of unused cryptographic code (SM2 + SM3) increases complexity, testing surface, and audit scope.

3. **Incompatible with TRON network**: TRON uses `SECP256K1` for all transactions. SM2 would not work with existing accounts.

4. **Bug Bounty false positives**: The unused SM2 code frequently triggers false positive reports in bug bounty programs. Security researchers often flag SM2-related code as potential vulnerabilities, requiring time-consuming investigation and response.

5. **Unnecessary configuration complexity**: The `crypto.engine` config and `isECKeyCryptoEngine` checks exist throughout the codebase but only serve unused code paths.

## Rationale

### Why should this feature exist?

Removing unused code improves code quality and reduces maintenance overhead. Eliminating the `crypto.engine` configuration simplifies the codebase by removing conditional logic that never changes in production.

### What are the use cases?

1. Developers will have cleaner codebase to work with
2. Security audits will focus on actually-used cryptography
3. Fewer false positive reports in bug bounty programs
4. Simplified code with no unnecessary conditional branches

## Scope of Impact

- [ ] Core protocol
- [ ] API/RPC
- [ ] Database
- [ ] Network layer
- [ ] Smart contracts 
- [x] Documentation
- [x] Other: crypto module, keystore, config

**Breaking Changes:**

Yes - but only for non-production code paths:
- `crypto.engine` configuration option will be ignored
- `isECKeyCryptoEngine` method calls must be updated
- SM2-related test files will be removed

**Backward Compatibility:**

Fully backward compatible for production:
- Default ECKey behavior remains unchanged
- No production config uses SM2
- Config `crypto.engine` can remain in old configs (will be ignored with warning)


## Implementation

**Are you willing to implement this feature?**
- [x] Yes, I can implement this feature

items.title: RAGE Streaming Budget (grcResourceCache) Saturates to 100% From Base Game Assets Alone — Build-Independent, Reproducible on Localhost With Zero Custom Content

labels.name: triage
labels.description: Needs a preliminary assessment to determine the urgency and required action

items.body: ### What happened? # RAGE Streaming Budget (grcResourceCache) Saturates to 100% From Base Game Assets Alone — Build-Independent, Reproducible on Localhost With Zero Custom Content ## Summary The RAGE streaming pool (`grcore/ResourceCache`) fills to 99.7–100% capacity from base game assets alone, with zero custom streamed content. This is reproducible on localhost with no server infrastructure involved. The behavior is **not build-dependent** — tested on builds 3095 (The Chop Shop), 3258 (Bottom Dollar Bounties), and 3570 (Money Fronts), all producing the same saturation. The budget saturates on all tested VRAM tiers (10 GB, 12 GB, 16 GB), at any Extended Texture Budget slider setting, and does not release while stationary. On ≤12 GB VRAM cards, this results in synchronous evict-load stalls causing 0.5–2 second client freezes during area transitions. ## Reproduction Steps **Environment — minimal localhost, zero custom content:** - FiveM localhost (no remote server, no server artifact dependency) - Game builds tested: **3095** (The Chop Shop), **3258** (Bottom Dollar Bounties), **3570** (Money Fronts) — all produce the same saturation - OneSync: enabled - Custom streamed assets: **NONE** — Lua script resources only, no maps, no vehicles, no clothing, no MLOs - No `increase_pool_size` overrides relevant to streaming - No streaming-related convars in server.cfg **Steps:** 1. Launch FiveM with `+set moo 31338`, connect to localhost 2. Open F8 console, run `strmem` 3. Observe `grcore/ResourceCache` starting at ~2,200–3,500 MiB after spawn 4. Drive or walk through the base game city — no specific route required, normal traversal through Los Santosa Traversal via noclip accelerates the saturation due to covering more map area in less time, but the same saturation occurs with normal on-foot/vehicle movement — it simply takes longer to reach ceiling. 5. Observe `grcore/ResourceCache` climbing to 99.7–100% of its budget ceiling 6. Stop moving and stand still 7. Observe the budget remaining pinned at ceiling with no meaningful eviction (only ~5 MiB fluctuation from LOD mip swaps) **This has been reproduced on:** | GPU | VRAM | Budget Ceiling (approx) | Saturated After Traversal? | |-----|------|------------------------|---------------------------| | RTX 5080 Laptop | 16 GB | ~9,639 MiB | Yes — 9,607 / 9,639 (99.7%) | | RTX 4070 | 12 GB | ~8,460 MiB | Yes — 8,460 / 8,460 (99.99%) | | RTX 3080 | 10–12 GB | ~7,000–8,000 MiB | Yes (100%) — severe freezes | **Lowering the Extended Texture Budget slider** reduces the ceiling but the pool still fills to 100% of whatever budget is allocated. With a reduced budget on the RTX 5080, usage went from 0 → 3,520 / 3,520 MiB (100%) from the same city traversal. ## Top Streaming Consumers (All Base Game / DLC Assets) From the `strmem` output on the stripped localhost at budget saturation — no custom content present: | Asset | Total (MiB) | Source | |-------|-------------|--------| | cs5_3_cs5_03_pwrst+hi.ytd | 57.02 | Base game city texture | | globalroads+hi.ytd | 57.02 | Base game roads | | core.ypt | 57.14 | Base game particle system | | cs6_06_land.ytd | 49.53 | Base game city texture | | hei_dt1_carrmp.ytd | 38.77 | Cayo Perico Heist DLC | | ch_chint03_dlc_plan.ytd | 37.03 | Casino DLC interior | | platform:/textures/fxdecal.ytd | 33.02 | Base game | | ch_chint01_vwint01_vwdlc_vw_casino.ytd | 23.77 | Casino DLC interior | | ch_chint06_dlc_casino_back.ytd | 21.52 | Casino DLC interior | | ch_chint08_dlc_casino_loading.ytd | 21.01 | Casino DLC interior | | ch_chint07_dlc_casino_hotel.ytd | 20.27 | Casino DLC interior | | ch_chint09_dlc_vault.ytd | 20.27 | Casino DLC interior | | tr_sc1_02_tuner_hd.ydr | 24.07 | Tuner DLC | | m24_1_mp2024_01_additions_carrmp.ytd | 22.02 | Bottom Dollar Bounties DLC | Casino DLC interior textures alone consume approximately 144+ MiB from proximity preloading — without the player entering the interior. ## Key Behavioral Observations 1. **No proactive eviction:** Once assets are loaded, the RAGE streamer does not release them based on distance or time. Eviction only occurs under demand pressure when the budget is full and a new asset needs to load. Standing still after traversal results in the budget remaining permanently pinned at ceiling. 2. **Demand always exceeds supply and is not build-dependent:** Tested on builds 3095, 3258, and 3570 — all produce the same saturation behavior. The cumulative base game asset manifest from normal city traversal exceeds whatever budget ceiling is allocated, regardless of game build, VRAM tier, or Extended Texture Budget setting. Lowering the budget does not reduce demand — it forces lower mip selection and more aggressive eviction churn. Changing to an older build with fewer DLC packs does not prevent saturation. 3. **Tested across multiple server configurations — all saturate:** | Server | Custom Content | sv_pureLevel | Result | |--------|---------------|--------------|--------| | Production RP server (rogueMAIN) | Full (15.7 GB custom assets — vehicles, clothing, maps, MLOs) | Off | Saturates to 100% | | Dev server (rogueDEV) | Full custom content | Off | Saturates to 100% | | New stripped server | Zero custom streaming assets (Lua scripts only) | Off | Saturates to 100% | | Localhost | Zero custom streaming assets | N/A | Saturates to 100% | | Third-party server (Dream RP) | Heavy custom content, custom MLOs, build 3258 | On (`sv_pureLevel`) | Confirmed to saturate to budget ceiling after extended traversal. Affected user's system RAM climbed from 50% to 75% during this session. | ## Source Code Review - Only for reference what we noticed - not stating direct connection: We reviewed the relevant source in the public repository: -- Just observation - not making any connection or link to these just FYI... **`code/components/gta-streaming-five/src/PatchExtendedBudgeting.cpp`:** - `SetGamePhysicalBudget()` writes budget values to RAGE's internal VRAM threshold lookup table (`g_vramLocation`, 80 entries) - The `vid_budgetScale` convar (Extended Texture Budget slider) applies a multiplier: `(value / 12.0) + 1.0` - Base budget is set to `3 * GB` (3,000 MiB) for normal systems - `_getAvailableMemoryForStreamer` hook returns 0 when the loaded list exceeds 31,500 entries — this prevents new loads but does not trigger eviction - **No proactive eviction logic exists in FiveM's streaming hooks.** FiveM controls the budget ceiling and monitors usage, but the eviction algorithm is entirely within the GTA V executable **`code/components/gta-streaming-five/src/TextureStreamingLimits.cpp`:** - Introduces `str_maxVehicleTextureRes` (default 1024) and `str_maxVehicleTextureResRgba` (default 512) - Only applies to vehicle textures (`CVehicleModelInfo`), not to base game DLC interiors, clothing, or map textures **Last modification to `PatchExtendedBudgeting.cpp`: November 12, 2022** (commit `76f4a54` — increased the budget divisor from 8.0 to 12.0). No changes in over 3 years. ## Potentially Related Recent Commit **Commit `6487002` — December 22, 2025 — `fix(nui/core): use raw D3D11 device for DUI renderTarget`** Commit message states: *"Resolves referencing issue that incorrectly counted towards (and failed to deallocate) grcResourceCache total size. This caused the creation and deletion of dozens of DUI's to eventually lead into SLOD2 issues as the game incorrectly assumed that there was no available space for assets."* This commit directly modified how D3D11 resources interact with `grcResourceCache` budget accounting. Our server community began noticing increased client stuttering and freezes approximately late 2025 / early 2026. We did not have `strmem` data from before this period, so we cannot confirm whether the budget saturation behavior existed previously or is a recent change. We note the timing alignment but cannot confirm a causal relationship. ## Affected User Data **User 1 (severe freezes):** - RTX 3080, 10–12 GB VRAM - 2560×1440 + 1080p dual monitor - i9-12900F, 32 GB RAM, SSD - Windows 11 Home 10.0.26200 - Driver: Game Ready 591.86 **User 2 (freezes):** - RTX 4070, 12 GB VRAM - 2560×1440, single monitor - i9-13900K, 32 GB RAM, dual SSD - Windows 11 Home 10.0.26200 - Driver: Game Ready 595.79 **Operator (micro-stutters only, no severe freezes):** - RTX 5080 Laptop, 16 GB VRAM - Windows 11 Pro - Driver: Game Ready 591.86 Two different driver versions (591.86 and 595.79) and two different GPU generations (Ampere, Ada Lovelace) are affected. The common variable is VRAM capacity, not driver version or GPU architecture. ## Supporting Profiler Data A Chrome DevTools-style client profiler trace captured on an affected machine showed: - Post-DrawFrame gaps of 1.63s (48.696s → 50.328s) and 1.72s (73.104s → 74.828s) - No corresponding Lua/resource tick spike preceding the gaps - Pattern: Resource Manager Tick completes → DrawFrame → trace goes silent → next BeginFrame after 1.6–1.7s This is consistent with the CPU blocking at `Present()` while the GPU services VRAM page faults or the RAGE streamer performs synchronous eviction at budget ceiling. ## System Memory Log A 66-minute session log from User 1 (RTX 3080) on the production server (with custom assets) showed: - GTA Process working set: 9,093 MB → 10,486 MB (monotonic climb, no release) - ChromeBrowser (NUI): 1,545 MB → 1,197 MB (declining — NUI accumulation ruled out) - Pages/sec spike at minute 7.8: 13,352 pages/sec (~52 MB/sec of page fault servicing) ## Expected Behavior The streaming pool should not remain permanently saturated at 100% during normal gameplay. Possible approaches: 1. A proactive eviction pass that releases assets from areas the player has left, maintaining headroom (e.g., targeting 80–85% utilization rather than 100%) 2. Distance-based deprioritization of DLC interior preloads when the player is outside the interior trigger zone 3. Review of whether the `grcResourceCache` accounting changes in commit `6487002` have downstream effects on eviction behavior ## Related Commits and Discussions - [`c42ebdb`](https://github.com/citizenfx/fivem/commit/c42ebdbfde2c040c2731cd5ddd51e7e591bb41ee) — 2021-06-17 — feat: extended video memory budget option - [`079e62e`](https://github.com/citizenfx/fivem/commit/079e62e3e71556841970211570c39309758a3796) — 2022-09-10 — fix: show accurate VRAM impact for extended budget - [`76f4a54`](https://github.com/citizenfx/fivem/commit/76f4a548c783fba21e9ac3da36f52b7873730097) — 2022-11-12 — tweak: increase extended budget divisor - [`6487002`](https://github.com/citizenfx/fivem/commit/64870025b4d3bc154456a12216f960e5be5d0f76) — 2025-12-22 — fix: use raw D3D11 device for DUI renderTarget (grcResourceCache accounting change) - [Forum: nta response about VRAM budget cap](https://forum.cfx.re/t/ui-update/4945741) — 2022-11-08 - [Forum: Extended Texture Budget slider cap report](https://forum.cfx.re/t/extended-texture-budget/5321311) — 2025-04-22 [fivem_memory_log (1).csv](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26106089/fivem_memory_log.1.csv) [fivem_memory_chart.html](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26106091/fivem_memory_chart.html) ### Expected result ## Expected Behavior The streaming pool should not remain permanently saturated at 100% during normal gameplay. Possible approaches: 1. A proactive eviction pass that releases assets from areas the player has left, maintaining headroom (e.g., targeting 80–85% utilization rather than 100%) 2. Distance-based deprioritization of DLC interior preloads when the player is outside the interior trigger zone 3. Review of whether the `grcResourceCache` accounting changes in commit `6487002` have downstream effects on eviction behavior ### Reproduction steps **Steps:** 1. Launch FiveM with `+set moo 31338`, connect to localhost 2. Open F8 console, run `strmem` 3. Observe `grcore/ResourceCache` starting at ~2,200–3,500 MiB after spawn 4. Drive or walk through the base game city — no specific route required, normal traversal through Los Santosa Traversal via noclip accelerates the saturation due to covering more map area in less time, but the same saturation occurs with normal on-foot/vehicle movement — it simply takes longer to reach ceiling. 5. Observe `grcore/ResourceCache` climbing to 99.7–100% of its budget ceiling 6. Stop moving and stand still 7. Observe the budget remaining pinned at ceiling with no meaningful eviction (only ~5 MiB fluctuation from LOD mip swaps) ### Importancy Crash ### Area(s) FiveM ### Specific version(s) FiveM Windows Client 26197 ### Additional information _No response_

items.body:
---
name: 💰 Bounty - Media Stack
about: 实现媒体服务栈 (Jellyfin, Sonarr, Radarr, Prowlarr, qBittorrent, Jellyseerr)
title: '[BOUNTY $200] Media Stack — 媒体服务栈'
labels: bounty, medium
assignees: ''
---

## 赏金金额

**$200 USDT** (或等值法币)

## 任务描述

实现完整的媒体服务栈，包含以下服务：

| 服务 | 镜像 | 用途 |
|------|------|------|
| Jellyfin | `jellyfin/jellyfin:10.9.11` | 媒体服务器 |
| Sonarr | `lscr.io/linuxserver/sonarr:4.0.11` | 剧集管理 |
| Radarr | `lscr.io/linuxserver/radarr:5.8.1` | 电影管理 |
| Prowlarr | `lscr.io/linuxserver/prowlarr:1.22.0` | 索引器管理 |
| qBittorrent | `lscr.io/linuxserver/qbittorrent:4.6.7` | 下载器 |
| Jellyseerr | `fallenbagel/jellyseerr:2.1.1` | 请求管理 |

## 文件结构

```
stacks/media/
├── docker-compose.yml
├── .env.example
└── README.md
```

## 要求

### 1. 目录结构

遵循 [TRaSH Guides](https://trash-guides.info/Hardlinks/How-to-setup-for/Docker/) 硬链接最佳实践：

```
/data/
├── torrents/
│   ├── movies/
│   └── tv/
└── media/
    ├── movies/
    └── tv/
```

### 2. 环境变量

通过 `.env` 管理：

- `MEDIA_ROOT` — 媒体目录
- `DOWNLOADS_ROOT` — 下载目录
- `PUID` / `PGID` / `TZ`
- 各服务密码

### 3. Traefik 配置

- 每个服务暴露子域名：`jellyfin.${DOMAIN}`, `sonarr.${DOMAIN}`, ...
- HTTPS 自动证书
- 可选：Authentik Forward Auth 保护

### 4. 健康检查

每个容器必须有 `healthcheck` 配置。

### 5. 启动顺序

`depends_on` + `condition: service_healthy` 确保正确启动顺序。

### 6. README 内容

- 服务功能说明
- 启动命令
- 目录结构说明
- Sonarr/Radarr 连接 qBittorrent 的配置步骤
- Jellyfin 媒体库添加步骤
- 常见问题 (FAQ)

## 验收标准

- [ ] `docker compose up -d` 成功启动所有 6 个服务
- [ ] 所有服务健康检查通过 (`docker compose ps` 显示 healthy)
- [ ] Traefik 反代生效，各子域名可访问
- [ ] Sonarr 可以搜索剧集并触发 qBittorrent 下载
- [ ] Jellyfin 识别 `/data/media` 中的媒体库
- [ ] README 文档完整
- [ ] 无硬编码密码/密钥

## 认领方式

在下方评论 "我来认领"，确认后开始开发。

## 支付

验收通过后 3 个工作日内支付 USDT (TRC20) 或等值法币。

items.title: Add Your Name to CONTRIBUTORS.md — 5 RTC (Good First Issue)
items.body:
## Bounty: Add Your Name to CONTRIBUTORS.md — 5 RTC

**Difficulty:** Beginner (no coding required)
**Reward:** 5 RTC
**Time:** ~15 minutes

This bounty teaches you the full GitHub contribution workflow — fork, edit, pull request. Once you've done this, you'll know how to contribute to any open source project.

---

### Requirements

- A GitHub account (free at github.com)
- Be a member of the RustChain Discord server
- That's it!

---

### Step-by-Step Instructions

**Step 1: Fork the repository**
1. Go to https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain
2. Click the **"Fork"** button in the top-right corner
3. This creates your own copy of the repo under your GitHub account

**Step 2: Navigate to CONTRIBUTORS.md**
1. In YOUR forked repo (it will say `YourUsername/Rustchain` at the top)
2. Click on the file `CONTRIBUTORS.md`
3. Click the **pencil icon** (Edit this file) in the top-right of the file view

**Step 3: Add your entry**
1. Scroll to the bottom of the file
2. Add a new line following this format:

```
| @YourGitHubUsername | YourDiscordHandle#0000 | What you're interested in (e.g., mining, development, testing, community) |
```

For example:
```
| @coolguy42 | CoolGuy#1234 | Interested in mining on vintage hardware |
```

**Step 4: Commit your changes**
1. Scroll down to "Commit changes"
2. In the commit message, write: `Add @YourUsername to CONTRIBUTORS.md`
3. Select **"Create a new branch for this commit and start a pull request"**
4. Click **"Propose changes"**

**Step 5: Create the Pull Request**
1. You'll be taken to the "Open a pull request" page
2. Title: `Add @YourUsername to CONTRIBUTORS.md`
3. In the description, write: `Claiming bounty from issue #XX` (replace XX with this issue number)
4. Click **"Create pull request"**

**Step 6: Claim your bounty**
1. Come back to THIS issue and leave a comment with a link to your PR
2. Once merged, 5 RTC will be sent to your wallet

---

### How to Get Paid

After your PR is merged, provide your RTC wallet address in the comments. If you don't have one yet:
```
pip install clawrtc
clawrtc wallet create
```

---

### FAQ

**Q: What if CONTRIBUTORS.md doesn't exist yet?**
A: Create it! That's even more impressive. Use the format above with a header row.

**Q: I messed up my fork, what do I do?**
A: Delete your fork and re-fork. No harm done.

**Q: Can multiple people claim this?**
A: Yes! Every person who adds themselves gets 5 RTC. This bounty stays open.

---

*This is a recurring bounty — it stays open for all new contributors.*

Recruit new people to create bots on BoTTube. This is an **ongoing** bounty — earn 50 RTC for every 10 new bot creators you bring in.

items.title: Find a Typo or Broken Link — 3 RTC (Good First Issue)
items.body:
## Bounty: Find and Report a Typo or Broken Link — 3 RTC

**Difficulty:** Beginner (no coding required)
**Reward:** 3 RTC per valid find (+ bonus 5 RTC if you submit a PR fixing it)
**Time:** ~10-20 minutes

Help us clean up the project! Every typo fixed and broken link repaired makes RustChain more professional.

---

### Requirements

- A GitHub account
- Be a member of the RustChain Discord server
- Basic reading skills (seriously, that's it)

---

### Where to Look

Check any of these repos for typos, broken links, grammar issues, or formatting problems:

| Repo | What to Check |
|------|---------------|
| [Scottcjn/Rustchain](https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain) | Main repo — README, docs, code comments |
| [Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties](https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties) | Bounty descriptions |
| [Scottcjn/bottube](https://github.com/Scottcjn/bottube) | BoTTube platform SDK |
| [Scottcjn/ram-coffers](https://github.com/Scottcjn/ram-coffers) | POWER8 research docs |

Also check:
- https://rustchain.org — the website
- https://pypi.org/project/clawrtc/ — PyPI package description

---

### Step-by-Step Instructions

**Step 1: Browse the repos**

Click through the files in any of the repos above. Read the README files, documentation, and code comments.

**Step 2: When you find something wrong, note it down**

Look for:
- **Typos**: misspelled words, wrong punctuation
- **Broken links**: URLs that go to 404 pages
- **Formatting issues**: broken markdown tables, missing headers
- **Outdated info**: instructions that no longer work
- **Grammar errors**: awkward sentences, wrong tense

**Step 3: Report your find as a comment on this issue**

Format:
```
**Repo:** Scottcjn/Rustchain
**File:** README.md
**Line:** 42
**Issue:** "recieve" should be "receive"
```

Or for broken links:
```
**Repo:** Scottcjn/Rustchain
**File:** docs/SETUP.md
**Line:** 15
**Issue:** Link to https://example.com/old-page returns 404
**Suggested fix:** Update to https://example.com/new-page
```

---

### Bonus: +5 RTC for Submitting a Fix

Know how to make a pull request? Fix the typo yourself!

1. Fork the repo (click "Fork" button)
2. Edit the file (click the pencil icon)
3. Fix the typo
4. Commit with message: `Fix typo in FILENAME`
5. Create a pull request
6. Link your PR in your comment here

That earns you the base **3 RTC** plus a **5 RTC bonus** = **8 RTC total**.

If you've never made a PR before, check out bounty issue #441 (Add Your Name to CONTRIBUTORS.md) first — it walks you through the entire process.

---

### Rules

- **One report per comment** — if you find 3 typos, post 3 separate comments (3 RTC each!)
- **Must be a real issue** — not a style preference

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: GitHub Webhook Receiver
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 200,000 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
Build the GitHub webhook handler that receives PR and issue events, updates bounty status in the database, and triggers the review pipeline.

### Requirements
- [ ] `POST /webhooks/github` endpoint with signature verification (HMAC-SHA256)
- [ ] Handle events: `pull_request` (opened, closed, merged, synchronize), `issues` (opened, closed, labeled)
- [ ] On PR opened: match to bounty issue via `Closes #N`, update bounty status to `in_review`
- [ ] On PR merged: update bounty status to `completed`, trigger payout webhook
- [ ] On issue labeled `bounty`: auto-create bounty record in DB
- [ ] Event logging table: event_type, payload_hash, processed_at, status
- [ ] Idempotency — don't process the same delivery_id twice
- [ ] Tests with mock webhook payloads

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + hmac verification

items.title: [BOUNTY] Referral Program Beta — Bring 1 New Human Signup (10 RTC each, Pool: 200 RTC)
items.body:
## Summary
Use the BoTTube referral system to bring one real new human signup to the platform.

## Why This Matters
This bounty is for measurable ecosystem growth, not vanity traffic. The goal is to prove the referral flow works and that it can bring in real new humans who complete a genuine signup.

## Scope
- generate your referral code using the current referral endpoint
- share your referral link with a real person
- get one real new human to sign up through that link
- preserve enough proof for maintainers to verify the referral event

## Referral Flow
- referral short-link: `https://bottube.ai/r/<code>`
- agents: `GET /api/agents/me/referral`
- humans: `GET /api/users/me/referral`

## Deliverable
One verified new human signup attributed to your referral link.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] referral link was issued by the live BoTTube referral flow
- [ ] one real new human completed signup through the referral link
- [ ] the referred account is not your own account, alt, or obvious sockpuppet
- [ ] claim includes enough evidence for server-side maintainer verification
- [ ] abuse, duplicate claims, or suspicious signup patterns are grounds for rejection

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- your referral link
- screenshot or recording of the signup confirmation without sensitive data
- the new user's BoTTube username or profile URL
- approximate signup time for maintainer cross-checking
- your RTC wallet or `miner_id`

## Payout
- `10 RTC` per accepted referral
- pool: `200 RTC`

## Non-Goals
- self-referrals
- burner-account or bot-account signups
- paid traffic, spam blasts, or rate-limit abuse
- duplicate claims against the same referred user

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for referral proof and payout review. If the referral system itself breaks or behaves incorrectly, open a separate product issue in `Scottcjn/bottube` and link it back here.

## Notes
If you hit rate limits, wait and retry normally. Do not brute force the referral endpoints.

# Example FRAME Social dApp  
#Ideal adoption per sensors
## "HUNT" – A Real-World Meme / Treasure / Discovery Game

This is a social media dApp designed for **instant adoption**.

It mixes:

- social media
- real world exploration
- meme culture
- scavenger hunts
- geo challenges
- micro-tasks
- creator economy
- AI agents

Think:

Pokémon Go  
+ Reddit  
+ TikTok  
+ scavenger hunts  
+ bounty economy

All running on FRAME.

---

# Core Idea

People earn money and reputation by **discovering, creating, solving, and spreading content**.

Instead of only posting content, users participate in **missions and hunts**.

Examples:

- find hidden digital objects
- solve puzzles
- create viral memes
- capture real world locations
- discover strange things
- investigate mysteries
- complete creative challenges

Everything creates **verifiable receipts** so rewards are fair.

---

# Identity

Users join using their FRAME identity.

Identity includes:

- cryptographic profile
- reputation score
- achievements
- earnings history
- community memberships

Identity is portable.

Your followers and reputation move with you.

---

# The Map

The main interface is a **global map**.

The map shows:

- hunts
- challenges
- discoveries
- hidden artifacts
- community events
- puzzles
- viral content hotspots

Players explore the world digitally and physically.

---

# Hunts

A hunt is a challenge posted by anyone.

Example hunts:

- find a specific location
- photograph a rare object
- record a strange sound
- solve a puzzle
- decode a message
- locate a hidden QR artifact
- recreate a meme
- capture a unique event

Each hunt includes:

- reward
- rules
- verification logic

The first person to complete the hunt gets paid.

---

# Meme Economy

Memes become **competitive challenges**.

Example:

Someone posts:

"Create the funniest version of this meme"

Community votes.

Winner receives rewards.

Because posts are signed, the original creator is always known.

---

# Viral Challenge System

Users can start challenges like:

- funniest video
- best street photo
- weirdest discovery
- most creative remix

People participate and vote.

Rewards come from:

- sponsors
- advertisers
- community funding

---

# Discovery Mode

Users can post **discoveries**.

Examples:

- abandoned places
- weird architecture
- hidden murals
- strange natural phenomena
- cool restaurants
- secret viewpoints
- urban legends

Discoveries become permanent map objects.

People earn rewards if the discovery becomes popular.

---

# Bounties

Anyone can create a bounty.

Example:

Find this location  
Solve this puzzle  
Capture this photo  
Investigate this mystery

Users race to complete it.

The bounty creator deposits funds into a contract.

The winner receives payment automatically.

---

# AI Generated Hunts

Agents generate hunts automatically.

Examples:

- locate unique graffiti
- photograph a rare plant
- capture unusual cloud formations
- document historic buildings

Agents analyze submissions and verify authenticity.

---

# Verification

FRAME allows hunts to be verified using:

- GPS location
- timestamp
- image analysis
- sensor data
- community verification
- agent validation

This prevents cheating.

---

# Local Community Hunts

Cities can host events.

Example:

City tourism hunt.

Participants must:

- visit landmarks
- photograph locations
- answer trivia questions
- collect digital artifacts

Players earn rewards and achievements.

---

# Mystery Hunts

Communities can build complex puzzles.

Example:

ARG style mystery.

Players decode clues across:

- posts
- locations
- cryptography puzzles
- hidden objects

Winning teams receive large rewards.

---

# Creator Economy

Creators earn money by creating:

- hunts
- puzzles
- games
- community events
- meme challenges

Popular hunts generate revenue.

---

# Sponsor Hunts

Businesses can sponsor hunts.

Example:

Coffee shop hunt.

Players must:

- visit store
- photograph special item
- answer trivia

Players receive rewards.

Businesses gain foot traffic.

---

# Reputation

Players build reputation through:

- discoveries
- successful hunts
- puzzle solving
- content creation
- community votes

High reputation unlocks larger hunts.

---

# Global Leaderboards

Leaderboards include:

- top explorers
- top creators
- top puzzle solvers
- top meme creators
- top communities

---

# Digital Artifacts

Players can collect artifacts.

Examples:

- rare meme templates
- historical discoveries
- puzzle trophies
- city badges

Artifacts can be traded.

---

# AR Integration

Augmented reality can display:

- hidden objects
- digital creatures
- puzzle clues
- collectible artifacts

Players explore cities like a game.

---

# Real World Missions

Large missions could include:

- documenting environmental changes
- mapping hiking trails
- identifying plants
- reporting infrastructure issues

Users earn money for useful discoveries.

---

# Community Worlds

Communities can create themed worlds.

Examples:

- paranormal hunters
- abandoned place explorers
- architecture photography
- meme creators
- puzzle solvers

Each world has its own hunts and rewards.

---

# Money Flow

Money enters the system from:

- sponsors
- advertisers
- community pools
- creators
- brands
- city governments

Players earn rewards by participating.

---

# Why People Would Instantly Use It

It solves multiple things people already love:

People love:

- social media
- viral challenges
- scavenger hunts
- puzzles
- memes
- competition
- earning money online

This combines them into one platform.

---

# Why FRAME Makes It Better

FRAME enables:

- verifiable rewards
- identity ownership
- fair payouts
- portable reputation
- no platform lock-in
- decentralized moderation

Users keep their audience and earnings.

---

# Long Term Evolution

Over time the system becomes:

- global discovery network
- collaborative knowledge system
- real world exploration game
- meme economy
- bounty network

People earn money by **being curious, creative, and observant**.

---

# End Vision

The app turns the real world into a **giant interactive game**.

People explore cities.

Solve puzzles.

Create memes.

Document strange discoveries.

Earn money.

And build communities around curiosity and creativity.
